title: MMALS core trunk
summary: The main architecture line: heterogeneous hosts, an active fungal medium, inferred context, functional route memory, and auditable continual learning.
claim: The validated core remains deliberately narrow: inferred-context routing, route-function memory, and energy/stability-aware auditing.
next: Broader datasets, hardened baselines, and cross-seed causal replication.

title: Functional route memory
summary: The route is not the memory object by itself; memory must preserve the mediated computation produced through the route.
claim: Route stability does not imply latent-function stability.
next: Measure when route, latent, output, transform, and replay constraints each add causal value.

title: Inferred-context MMALS
summary: Replace explicit task identity with q(c|x), propagate context uncertainty into routing, prediction, calibration, and action gating.
claim: The system should infer the situation before choosing routes.
next: Move from controlled overlap-chain contexts to broader class-incremental and task-free settings.

title: RC2O continual-learning evidence
summary: External benchmark bridge and evidence line spanning MNIST-family foundations, SplitCIFAR10, SplitCIFAR100, CORe50, selector regret, and regime audits.
claim: Retention and auditability must be qualified beyond toy task-ID settings.
next: Finish hardened baselines and broaden external datasets.

title: Stable–plastic context memory
summary: Dataset-agnostic context-memory fusion, deployed-posterior distillation, rescue ablations, and regression gates for difficult contexts.
claim: Context memory must preserve old competence without freezing context adaptation.
next: Resolve oldest-context failure modes and validate across seeds and datasets.

title: Geometry-MMALS
summary: Context manifolds, route-simplex dynamics, functional transport, curvature, perturbation maps, and causal host-specialization tests.
claim: Geometry should explain and improve routing and memory only where it survives causal and held-out tests.
next: Bridge isolation, common host banks, directional-prototype energy, SPD covariance, and memory transport.

title: Route–function–transform co-evolution
summary: Tests whether transformation geometry and local sensitivity drift add explanatory or regularization value beyond route and latent preservation.
claim: Transformation drift is a plausible mechanism, not yet a default policy.
next: Use warm, weak, decomposed regularizers and harder contexts without over-constraining plasticity.

title: Hydro-MMALS
summary: Fluid-inspired diagnostics for route drift, latent drift, output drift, adaptive viscosity, Reynolds-like instability, and turbulence-like behavior.
claim: A diagnostic mechanism contribution, not a default performance policy.
next: Replicate under stronger stress and test whether diagnostics predict failures early.

title: MMALS-CAL
summary: Competence, Coverage, Compactness, Context, and ACTION: regime-aware calibration and decision safety for continual learning.
claim: Calibration can restore coverage but cannot manufacture competence.
next: Complete cross-dataset evidence contracts and delayed-label regime verification.

title: CAL evidence contract
summary: Versioned CSV/JSONL schemas, ZIP delivery profiles, validators, adapters, and acceptance levels for reproducible CAL evaluation.
claim: Evidence should be portable, machine-validatable, and decoupled from a single notebook.
next: Publish the repository and connect notebook exporters.

title: MMALS-TPUT
summary: A minimal whole-system control loop unifying geometry, energy-guided routing, dual memory, goal-conditioned control, cost, and evidence.
claim: An MMALS element is evaluated by its contribution to the whole governed system, not only by isolated performance.
next: Implement the narrow reference loop and validate the RC2O-to-control bridge.

title: Goal-adaptive control
summary: Geo/RL/forward–backward control that selects routes under accuracy, retention, cost, drift, specialization, and changing objectives.
claim: Policy quality depends on the current goal and constraints, not a single universal score.
next: Qualify against stable baselines and avoid claiming positive backward transfer before evidence.

title: MMALS-QI
summary: Quantum-inspired density-state geometry for ambiguous functional routes, without claiming quantum hardware or quantum advantage.
claim: Mixed-state functional memory may better represent uncertainty over possible route-functions.
next: Defer implementation until inferred-context and comparative evidence are stronger.

title: Synthetic-data scaling and collapse
summary: Experiments and conceptual analysis of recursive synthetic-data scaling, collapse signals, governance, and links to MMALS memory and control.
claim: Synthetic scaling should be governed by evidence of diversity, competence, and collapse risk.
next: Connect collapse signals to CAL and TPUT decision policies.

title: Activity replay and audit UX
summary: Interactive replay of routing, specialization, drift, regime changes, mutualistic gain, cost signals, and evidence traces.
claim: Reconstructive audit memory should be explorable, not only stored.
next: Publish the first repository and connect evidence ZIPs.

title: Diderot MMALS/ML Wiki
summary: The living semantic, pedagogical, historical, and traceability layer connecting concepts, labs, events, branches, repositories, and publications.
claim: One canonical meaning, several reading levels, and explicit evidence status.
next: Publish to GitHub Pages and keep chronology and registries refreshed.

title: STRAT-Q and evidence governance
summary: Evidence-centric observability, traceability, confidence-constrained economics, and quality-intelligence governance adjacent to MMALS.
claim: Decision provenance and confidence constraints are part of system quality, not after-the-fact reporting.
next: Keep adjacent governance concepts linked without presenting them as MMALS core evidence.

title: Related machine-learning and identity labs
summary: Classical vision, biometric feature extraction, digital identity, synthetic data, Bayesian decision support, and other learning projects that inform but do not validate MMALS.
claim: Adjacent projects provide methods, datasets, and governance lessons; they are not MMALS experimental evidence.
next: Add explicit concept-level cross-links and keep boundaries visible.
